projectDescription: A UEFI DXE driver that enables Resizable BAR (PCIe Base Address Register resizing) on systems that don't officially support it, unlocking GPU performance improvements on older motherboards.
keyFeatures: Enables Resizable BAR (ReBAR) on UEFI systems without official support
UEFI DXE driver injected into firmware DXE volume for automatic execution on every boot
Hooks PciHostBridgeResourceAllocationProtocol to configure BAR sizes during PCI enumeration
ReBarState utility for Windows and Linux to configure maximum BAR size via NVRAM variable
UEFIPatch patches to fix 64-bit BAR handling limitations in older UEFI firmware
CMOS reset detection to safely disable ReBAR if system clock is reset
Support for BAR sizes from 1MB up to unlimited (2^32 MB) across up to 6 BARs per device
Compatible with Sandy Bridge through modern Intel platforms and AMD systems
publicApiSurface: rebarInit (EFI_HANDLE, EFI_SYSTEM_TABLE*) — DXE driver entry point
reBarSetupDevice (EFI_HANDLE, EFI_PCI_ROOT_BRIDGE_IO_PROTOCOL_PCI_ADDRESS) — configures ReBAR for a PCI device
ReBarState NVRAM variable (GUID: A3C5B77A-C88F-4A93-BF1C-4A92A32C65CE) — stores maximum BAR size (0=disabled, 1-31=2^x MB, 32=unlimited)
ReBarState CLI — interactive tool to read/write the ReBarState UEFI variable on Windows and Linux
buildffs.py — build script to compile ReBarDxe.efi and package it as an FFS module
UEFIPatch patches.txt — patch definitions for removing BAR size limits and fixing 64-bit BAR handling
